mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation via a lower discount rate and reduced funding stress, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month when the rate path is still declining.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upside acceleration in CPIAUCSL raises expected policy restraint and real discount rates, which depresses S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days after inflation prints cross a material upside threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with CPIAUCSL above 3.0% do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign remains positive for more than 40% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and softer future demand, which lowers near-term earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next month when unemployment is trending upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not negative after UNRATE rises 0.3pp in 3 months, or if PAYEMS does not fall or stall in the same windows and the signal still performs.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient consumer demand and supports revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when sales are expanding above trend.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth is positive for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS streaks are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the median forward return is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with firm HOUST indicates broad real activity and capacity utilization improving faster than housing weakness, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in INDPRO and HOUST do not coincide with above-zero 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend alongside stable or falling UNRATE confirms labor-market resilience, which supports consumption and earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE is flat or lower
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-flat condition fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if negative returns dominate despite labor strength.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains very low and CPIAUCSL accelerates, markets infer policy is behind the curve and real rates are falling, which can briefly support equities over the next 5 trading days before tighter policy expectations reprice.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1.0% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation above 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if low-rate, higher-inflation episodes do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign flips negative consistently once the trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: A slowing GDPC1 growth rate signals weakening aggregate demand and lower future profit growth, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter once quarterly GDP decelerates materially.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with GDPC1 growth below 1.5% are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the median return is positive.
